authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.24 - Condition of participation: Medical record services
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services via eCFR
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS hospital Conditions of Participation are binding public regulations for participating hospitals. This section defines confidentiality, authenticated orders, electronic notifications, and required medical-record contents, making it authoritative for the source records and closure evidence used in clinical workflow coordination.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Patient truth-set verification, order context review, result and consult artifact handling, electronic notification routing, and medical-record closure evidence
used_for: patient and order truth-set requirements
medical-record confidentiality and authorized access constraints
authenticated order, consult, lab, radiology, and follow-up-care evidence expectations

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 489.24 - Special responsibilities of Medicare hospitals in emergency cases
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services via eCFR
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS EMTALA regulations are binding public requirements for emergency screening, stabilizing treatment, and appropriate transfer. They are authoritative for urgent workflow items that must move to qualified clinical owners and for transfer-record expectations that the coordination lane must not improvise.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Urgent or safety-sensitive task recognition, clinical escalation, and transfer-record routing
used_for: immediate escalation when a task implies emergency or critical clinical action
boundary against administrative handling of screening, stabilization, or transfer decisions
record-package expectations when emergency transfer evidence is involved

authoritative_sources.title: Uses and Disclosures for Treatment, Payment, and Health Care Operations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the federal privacy regulator for HIPAA covered entities. Its guidance is authoritative for treatment, payment, and operations disclosure pathways, minimum necessary posture, and when PHI routing requires privacy escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI handling, internal and external message routing, consent or disclosure basis review, and privacy escalation
used_for: minimum necessary PHI routing expectations
treatment, payment, operations, consent, and release basis checks
privacy-sensitive refusal and escalation rules

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule (CMS-0057-F)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS is the federal payer regulator for Medicare, Medicaid, CHIP, and qualified health plan interoperability requirements. The final rule is authoritative for prior authorization status visibility, payer-provider exchange, and decision transparency dependencies that can block orders, referrals, and scheduling workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Authorization, eligibility, payer dependency, and referral or order readiness review
used_for: authorization dependency blocker handling
payer decision status as a routing dependency rather than lane-owned interpretation
payer-provider exchange evidence requirements

authoritative_sources.title: Improving Your Office Testing Process: A Toolkit for Rapid-Cycle Patient Safety and Quality Improvement
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ is the federal patient-safety and quality agency. This provider-facing toolkit is authoritative public operating guidance for office test ordering, specimen tracking, result receipt, patient notification, documentation, and follow-up-loop safeguards.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Order intake, result receipt, result notification, follow-up task tracking, and missed-loop escalation
used_for: closed-loop result follow-up stages
missing result or follow-up documentation failure modes
result notification routing without clinical interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: SAFER Guides - Using Health IT
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy/Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTP/ONC is the federal health IT authority. The SAFER Guides are recognized public guidance for safe EHR-supported clinician communication, clinical orders, diagnostic test result reporting, and follow-up workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: EHR-supported order, clinician communication, diagnostic result follow-up, and cross-system handoff safety review
used_for: EHR in-basket, tasking, order, result, and clinician communication workflow expectations
system-of-record and interoperability risk controls
safety escalation when EHR workflow gaps could cause patient harm

authoritative_sources.title: Closing the Referral Loop: Receipt of Specialist Report
authoritative_sources.publisher: eCQI Resource Center, Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS stewards this eCQM measure. It is authoritative for referral-loop closure evidence because it defines successful closure around receipt of a report from the clinician to whom the patient was referred.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Referral loop tracking, receiving-owner acceptance, specialist report receipt, and closure confirmation
used_for: referral-loop closure criteria
specialist report or procedural report evidence requirements
failure mode where a completed appointment is not enough to close clinical follow-up

authoritative_sources.title: Care Coordination Measures Atlas
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ's atlas is a recognized public measurement reference for healthcare care coordination. It is authoritative for concepts such as information transfer, accountability, handoff relationships, coordination gaps, and patient-safety-oriented failure modes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-team coordination, accountability mapping, handoff quality, and blocker management
used_for: handoff accountability expectations
coordination failure mode definition
evidence-backed communication and owner tracking

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and request classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that the request is clinical workflow coordination rather than licensed clinical, payer, privacy, IT, or coding work. Capture service line, urgency tier, requester, prior owner, intended action, and source-system references.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Patient and artifact truth-set verification
workflow_stages.description: Verify patient or member identifiers, encounter, order, referral, result, task, message, authorization, and privacy records from systems of record before deciding any route or closure state.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and authorization review
workflow_stages.description: Check eligibility, referral requirement, PCP referral, payer authorization, documented order context, required attachments, due time, and tenant prerequisites before downstream scheduling or follow-up routing proceeds.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Clinical-administrative boundary and safety triage
workflow_stages.description: Identify whether the item is routable administrative coordination or requires licensed clinical judgment, nursing triage, result interpretation, order changes, emergency handling, or urgent patient-safety escalation.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: PHI and privacy gate review
workflow_stages.description: Confirm whether the handoff, message, or packet has a documented treatment, payment, operations, consent, release, or tenant authority basis and limit routing to minimum necessary information.

workflow_stages.name: Owner routing and handoff packaging
workflow_stages.description: Produce a workflow routing note or handoff summary naming prior owner, next owner, destination queue, due time, prerequisites, source records, missing evidence, blocker state, and communication obligations.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Blocker, conflict, and stale ownership escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate duplicate owners, conflicting system states, stale work items, missing authorization, privacy blockers, payer or policy conflicts, and safety-sensitive tasks to the correct adjacent owner without assigning unsupported responsibility.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm closure only when required artifacts, receiving-owner acceptance, specialist report or response, result follow-up documentation, due-time disposition, and residual blocker state are documented.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: EHR in-basket item, task record, clinical message, and routing pool reference
Order, order context, ordering clinician, standing order or protocol reference, and authenticated order status
Result notification, laboratory or radiology report, critical result flag, follow-up instruction, and patient notification state
Referral order, reason for referral, referral shell, referral packet, specialist report, procedural report, consult note, and receiving-owner acceptance
Encounter note, problem or indication context, service line, urgency tier, due time, and turnaround expectation
Eligibility, coverage, PCP referral, authorization number, authorization status, payer decision record, and utilization-management blocker
Consent, release, PHI sensitivity indicator, privacy hold, disclosure basis, and minimum necessary routing note
Workflow routing note, handoff summary, dependency or blocker log, routing confirmation note, escalation summary, and closure confirmation
systems_of_record: EHR in-basket, tasking, orders, results, encounter, and referral modules
Scheduling, referral management, clinical messaging, patient access, and CRM workqueues
Payer portal, eligibility, prior authorization, utilization-management, coding, and financial-clearance systems when they affect routing
Document exchange, HIE, fax or e-fax, result interface, imaging, laboratory, and attachment repositories
Consent, release, privacy, audit-log, and disclosure-management repositories
Tenant policy repository for service-line ownership, urgency tiers, after-hours coverage, turnaround expectations, and closure criteria

allowed: Classify clinical workflow coordination requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
Validate source records, artifact completeness, owner roles, due times, authorization dependencies, privacy basis, and closure prerequisites
Determine the correct destination queue or owner when tenant rules and source records are sufficient
Produce workflow routing notes, handoff summaries, dependency or blocker logs, routing confirmations, escalation summaries, and closure confirmations
Escalate stale, conflicting, duplicate, privacy-sensitive, payer-sensitive, or safety-sensitive workflow states to documented adjacent owners
escalate: Need for licensed clinical judgment, diagnosis, treatment planning, order change, result interpretation, or clinical urgency determination
Critical result, emergency, or patient-safety signal requiring qualified clinical owner action
PHI disclosure basis, consent, release, minimum necessary scope, or sensitive-record posture is unclear
Payer policy, medical necessity, authorization sufficiency, utilization, coding, or coverage interpretation changes the answer
Public guidance, tenant policy, payer requirements, state law, or systems of record conflict materially
Destination owner or receiving-owner acceptance cannot be uniquely established
refuse: Request to alter a clinician order, interpret a diagnostic result, decide treatment, diagnose, or determine medical necessity
Request to close a clinical result, referral, or follow-up loop without documented receiving-owner acceptance and required evidence
Request to disclose PHI without a valid workflow basis or beyond minimum necessary detail
Request to treat a pending payer authorization, ambiguous eligibility state, or missing referral requirement as sufficient to proceed
Request to bypass urgent clinical escalation because the work appears administratively convenient

domain_failure_modes: The lane behaves like generic queue dispatch and ignores healthcare-specific source records, PHI handling, clinical urgency, owner roles, and closure criteria.
A result, referral, or follow-up loop is closed without receiving-owner acceptance or required report evidence, creating patient harm risk.
An urgent or critical result is routed administratively instead of escalated to a licensed clinical owner.
A coordinator changes an order, interprets a result, decides treatment, or determines medical necessity outside licensed authority.
PHI is disclosed outside the treatment team or without documented treatment, payment, operations, consent, release, or tenant authority basis.
A referral or order proceeds despite missing eligibility, authorization, PCP referral, payer requirement, or required documentation.
Conflicting EHR, scheduling, referral, payer, or messaging records are synthesized into a confident answer instead of escalated.
Tenant-specific adaptations override public clinical, privacy, payer, or closure boundaries without recording assumptions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
A result or referral arrives without enough context to route safely
Multiple teams appear to own the same follow-up action
An urgent task implies clinical judgment and must be escalated immediately
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

required_workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Service line, urgency tier, owner role, due time, documented order context, referral packet evidence, receiving-owner acceptance, eligibility, PCP referral, prior authorization, payer status, privacy or consent basis, and tenant closure criteria gate routing and closure.

required_workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
required_workflow_questions.answer: EHR orders, results, encounter, task, in-basket, referral, scheduling, clinical messaging, payer portal, eligibility, authorization, utilization, coding, privacy, consent, document exchange, HIE, lab, imaging, and tenant policy repositories establish the truth set.

required_workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops at diagnosis, treatment planning, result interpretation, order alteration, clinical urgency determination, emergency stabilization or transfer decisions, PHI disclosure interpretation, medical necessity, payer coverage, authorization sufficiency, coding, and utilization review.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific scope-of-practice, consent, minor access, and sensitive-record rules
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_plan: Require tenant jurisdiction and privacy policy retrieval before routing PHI-sensitive, consent-sensitive, or licensed-review-sensitive work.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific queue ownership, urgency tiers, turnaround expectations, and after-hours coverage
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_plan: Require tenant owner map and service-line policy retrieval before definitive routing or closure.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Payer contract, plan-specific authorization, referral, coding, and medical-necessity rules
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_plan: Treat payer facts as dependencies and route interpretation to prior authorization, utilization management, coding, or financial-clearance owners.
